QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: National Pension System (NPS)_100

question.text: Amit has NPS Tier I corpus of Rs.30 lakh at age 60. He wants maximum lump sum. How much must he allocate to annuity at minimum?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** At normal exit (age 60), minimum 40% must go to annuity. 40% of Rs.30 lakh = Rs.12 lakh. The remaining Rs.18 lakh (60%) can be taken as tax-free lump sum. **The Trap:** 80% annuity is for premature exit. At normal exit, only 40% annuity is required. 40% of 30L = 12L. **Cross-Exam Context:** Normal exit: 40% annuity (min), 60% lump sum (max). Corpus > Rs.5 lakh so full lump sum not applicable. Rs.12 lakh minimum annuity. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Walk clients through the exact math before retirement. Rs.12 lakh annuity at current rates would yield approximately Rs.6,000-8,000 monthly pension depending on type.
